title: Robinsonia berteroi
taxonomy: Robinsonia berteroi belongs to the family Asteraceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It is a member of the tribe Senecioneae, which includes a wide range of species with similar characteristics. Within the Senecioneae tribe, Robinsonia berteroi is classified as a species of the genus Robinsonia. The genus Robinsonia is a small group of plants native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Further classification of Robinsonia berteroi may involve its placement within a specific species group or infraspecific rank.

intro: Robinsonia berteroi, also known as Guanacaste or Guayacan, is a shrub or small tree native to the Caribbean and Central America, belonging to the family Lamiaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with health benefits including the treatment of fever, cough, and gastrointestinal issues, as well as its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The bioactive constituents of Robinsonia berteroi include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic activities. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, with the most common preparations being teas and infusions for coughs and fever.
